samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data.

For each serial output, the module outputs the most significant bit of the parallel input first, followed by the remaining bits in order of decreasing significance.

Specifically, when valid_out=1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output in the following three cycles.

For example, if the input data is 1101, the output will be 1101 in the first four cycles, and then in the fifth cycle, the valid_out will be 1 and the dout will be 1, and in the sixth cycle, the valid_out will be 1 and the dout will be 0, and so on.
